notes: SHIP v1.14.249 — BOUNDARY FIX: net-6x100g-02 (MASTER_U_TABLE, one line, data-only). Closes a latent model-exactness leak found while scoping the media-converter ruling. THE RULE LIED ABOUT ITSELF: .242 seeded `{ re: /net-6x100g-02/, u: 1 }` as a BARE SUBSTRING with no boundary, while its own comment claimed 'KEYED TO EXACT REV -02: a future -03 is different hardware until its own pitch proves otherwise.' The regex did not enforce that. Any longer SKU containing the substring silently inherited John's 1U ruling. ⚠️ NOTE `\b` WOULD NOT HAVE FIXED IT EITHER — \b matches BEFORE a hyphen (the exact .247 q3400-ra trap). SHIPPED: `{ re: /(^|[^a-z0-9-])net-6x100g-02([^a-z0-9-]|$)/, u: 1 }` — the class excludes a leading/trailing `-` and any alphanumeric, so only the exact rev John ruled on can match. The haystack in master_nodeHeightInfo is `model + ' ' + dns`, and a SPACE is a boundary char under this class, so real hosts still hit. GUARD (both real masters replayed through the SHIPPED table vs the .248 baseline): HEIGHT DRIFT = 0 across ALL 6490 placed hosts on BOTH masters — behaviour on real data is bit-identical. The 6 real net-6x100g-02 hosts (SPARKS s3:175/s3:176) still resolve {u:1, known:true}. THREE LEAKS CLOSED (each was 1U-known under .248, now honestly unknown): `net-6x100g-02-x` · `net-6x100g-021` (a FAR more plausible future rev than the -02-x that first raised the flag — the bare substring would have silently given a brand-new NetBox rev John never saw a ruling it never earned) · `xnet-6x100g-02` (prefix leak). Controls unchanged: `net-6x100g-03` / `net-6x100g-04` / `net-6x100g` were unknown before and remain unknown. This is the same discipline as .247 and .242: seeding a family the owner did not rule on is precisely the guess .238 exists to kill — and the correct generalisation is that NEITHER a bare substring NOR `\b` is a model-exactness boundary when the SKU contains hyphens. ⚠️ SIBLING RULES NOT TOUCHED (strict scope, flagged for John's call): `{ re: /ngfw-4245/ }` is also a bare substring, and `{ re: /\bas-?2125/ }` has no trailing boundary — both carry the same latent leak. Neither is fixed here because neither was asked for and no drive-by changes. Say the word and they go in one ship. Type-only ships (.245/.247) and this height-only ship remain strictly separable: this touches HEIGHT only, no type moved. Gates: node --check x4 inline blocks + sw.js PASS, CSS brace balance 3856/3856 IDENTICAL (no CSS this ship), CRLF preserved (bare-LF 0), three-stamp lockstep .248 -> .249. ?legacy=1: unaffected — MASTER_U_TABLE is a pure data table behind a pure classifier, no markup, no gating, no CSS. BATCH = 5 (.245 .246 .247 .248 .249), cap 6 — ⚠️ ONE SHIP OF HEADROOM LEFT. .245/.246/.247/.248 all still UNVERIFIED. DEVICE-VERIFY (John), one pass covers all five: [.249] SPARKS s3:175/s3:176 -> the 6 net-6x100g-02 rows (pkey01/pkey03/metal-ztp01 family) still draw as 1U, to scale, NO height hatch — identical to .248. Nothing else on either master may change height. [.248] BUILD subtab strip -> Crash-Cart pill GONE; tap Rack Map and Optics (the pills that flanked it) — neither dead-taps nor lands on SOPs; same under ?legacy=1 (pill gone there too, signed). [.247] s1:001 -> q3400-ra reads SWITCH + still gold hatch + MODEL HEIGHT UNKNOWN. [.246] s3:176 -> U31/U29/U28 read UNKNOWN gold, solid + to scale, no hatch; no tray prints 'undefined'. [.245] s3:176 U27 UFM reads SERVER. STILL OWED, JOHN'S RULING: the MEDIA-CONVERTER TYPE — net-6x100g-02 x6 and fs-media-converter-chassis x5 are BOTH media converters (John, 2026-07-14) but the app has NO media-converter type (gpu/switch/pdu/patch/server/storage/unknown/blank); awaiting his type + colour call. Heights split and must NOT be inferred from the type ruling: net-6x100g-02 is 1U known (this ship), fs-media-converter-chassis is height-unknown (stays hatched).
